danger_signals.visual_form: Boss character model, criminal underworld aesthetic
danger_signals.meaning: Primary antagonist and source of conflict; represents organized crime threat
Escalating danger as player progresses through tournament tiers toward final boss
Lawless territory controlled by hostile faction; perpetual danger environment
Immediate tactical danger indicator during combat

safety_signals.visual_form: Arm wrestling machine visual, non-combat interlude
safety_signals.meaning: Brief respite between dangerous tournament matches; recovery opportunity
Emotional anchor and justification for hero actions; represents lost safety

safety_signals.visual_form: Terry, Andy, and Joe Higashi playable character selection
safety_signals.meaning: Allied support and collective strength against threats

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Martial arts legacy, Hakkyokuseiken and Shiranui-ryū fighting styles
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Contrasted against Geese Howard's criminal aesthetic
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Street fighting origins in South Town; self-taught/mentored warrior appearance

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Crime boss authority; 'King of Fighters' tournament sponsorship
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Ruthless businessman martial artist; merciless crime boss aesthetic
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: South Town criminal underworld control; tournament venue hosting authority
Thai/Japanese cultural martial arts representation; foreign alliance

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Character introduction sequences, grave visitation scenes, South Town urban environments
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Characters gathering information about KOF tournament; motivation establishment through narrative

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Stage-by-stage progression through opponent gauntlet; escalating difficulty visualization
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Continuous combat against CPU opponents in best-of-three matches; two-lane battle field transitions

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: South Town setting under Geese Howard's control; underworld-themed stage designs
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Lawless environment; enemies represent criminal faction threats; no law enforcement presence

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Geese Howard as final boss; physical/visual representation of overwhelming antagonistic force
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Forced tournament progression to confront boss; hero characters must achieve training/strength first

environmental_storytelling.example: Jeff Bogard's murder and grave visitation establish emotional stakes and world conflict without explicit exposition
South Town urban environments visualize Geese Howard's criminal dominion; tournament venues embody his authority
Ascending opponent difficulty and bonus rounds create pacing that communicates journey toward final confrontation
Bogard brothers' martial artist appearance, Joe Higashi's Muay Thai identity, and Geese's ruthless businessman aesthetic convey faction allegiances visually
Multiple combat spaces suggest complex South Town environments with foreground/background spatial depth

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Wandering self-training; mastering Hakkyokuseiken; returns to confront Geese after decade of preparation
Training in Japan; perfecting distinct martial art (Shiranui-ryū Ninjutsu); returns home for revenge mission
Befriends Bogard brothers after tournament discovery; agrees to join KOF fight against Geese
Organizes KOF tournament; rules South Town underworld; presents as martial arts expert combined with ruthless businessman
Engage in two-out-of-three matches; change rows in battle arenas; respond to player advancement through tournament brackets

notes: Fatal Fury communicates world-state primarily through narrative integration and character design rather than explicit UI elements typical of later fighting games. The core danger signal is Geese Howard's dominance over South Town and his criminal organization. Safety emerges through protagonist alliance formation and mentor legacy justification. Faction identity relies on martial arts style differentiation and character aesthetic rather than color-coding or symbolic markers. The two-lane battle mechanic itself communicates environmental complexity of South Town spaces. World-state progression follows classical revenge narrative: injury establishment → hero preparation → tournament entry → final confrontation. Political situation (crime boss rule) is implied rather than explicitly visualized through stage design and NPC behavior.
